$schema: mlr-app architecture map — a single reference artifact for AI agents and developers working on this codebase (web + the native iOS client)

meta.generatedBy: Full-refresh pass (superseding the 2026-07-22 version, which had drifted materially — see changeLog) — direct reads of CLAUDE.md, package.json, supabase/migrations/*.sql filenames, components/*.tsx, lib/*.ts, app/**/page.tsx, plus an Explore-agent survey of the mlr-app-ios repo
meta.purpose: A private, family-only PWA for a small family lake resort/club near Tomahawk, WI (EST 1987). NOT a commercial booking product — closed-community social software: browse public, verified email unlocks interaction (posts/chat/RSVP/polls/tournaments/etc).
meta.audience: An extended family of mixed ages and technical ability, organized into 'houses' (family branches) and volunteer 'committees'. Admins are promoted family members, not staff.
meta.howToUse: `architecture` maps the app as-is (routes, nav, providers, data layer, ~65-table data model, components, backend, chat internals). `majorFeatures` documents every substantial feature area added since the last architecture pass, each tagged with its iOS-parity status. `iosParity` is the payload built specifically for opening Xcode on mlr-app-ios — repo structure, what's built, what's a confirmed gap, and doc-vs-reality drift to watch for. `designSystem` + `motionKit` describe the current (now-shipped) look/motion. `hardConstraints` are rules that must never be broken. `improvementOpportunities` is the remaining backlog (many prior items shipped — see status field on each).
meta.twoClients: One Supabase backend (project vrksrpzlslrcjvbzchfg) serves TWO clients: this Next.js web PWA (Vercel) AND a native SwiftUI iOS app (mlr-app-ios, iOS 18 minimum deployment, iOS 26 Liquid Glass features gated behind availability checks). Any schema/RPC/realtime change must preserve parity — a web-only change can silently break iOS, and vice versa. There is no separate iOS database.
meta.changeLog: Since the 2026-07-22 pass: framer-motion actually shipped (was 'planned', now installed ^12.42.2 + MotionProvider/AnimatedNumber/AnimatedList/SegmentedControl/Celebration/TypingIndicator all live); chat became optimistic with typing indicators (many prior 'high severity' chat gaps are now closed); component count grew 147 -> 168; migrations grew to 0154; five entire feature areas were undocumented and are now added: event sign-up slots, tournament brackets, private activities, quick polls in chat, meeting scheduling, conversation search, plus places-to-stay approver delegation and cabin guest messaging.
keyDocs: docs/ios-parity-2026-07.md / ios-committee-taxonomy-parity.md / ios-swiftui-strategy.md (iOS-repo-facing notes, see iosParity)

docs/DATA_MODEL.json — the exhaustive companion to THIS file: every table's exact columns/types, every RLS policy verbatim, every RPC's full signature/gate/grant, a feature-by-feature access matrix (who can read/create/edit/archive), and an objectDimensions section answering 'what media/photo/link fields exist on which object' — built by directly reading all 158 files in supabase/migrations/*.sql rather than summarizing prose. Read DATA_MODEL.json when you need ground-truth permissions or schema; read THIS file for the feature/nav/component map.

stack.styling: Tailwind v4 (CSS-variable theme tokens in app/globals.css @theme block)
stack.language: TypeScript (tsc --noEmit is the only static check; no ESLint — next lint removed in Next 16)
stack.backend: Supabase (Postgres + RLS + Realtime + Auth email-OTP + Storage)
stack.mediaBackend: Node/Express media-server on Brian's Mac mini (uploads, transcode, push, APNs, mailer, AI moderation, on-device semantic-search embeddings) — NOT Vercel
stack.motion: SHIPPED (was planned as of 2026-07-22, now live): framer-motion ^12.42.2 is the physics layer on top of the CSS motion tokens in globals.css. One global MotionProvider (components/MotionProvider.tsx, reducedMotion="user") wraps the app in layout.tsx. Primitives: AnimatedNumber (rAF count-up), AnimatedList (FLIP reorder), SegmentedControl (+ FamilyFestNav layoutId pill), Celebration (confetti), TypingIndicator. Wide-gamut P3/HDR color layer (@supports color(display-p3 …)) enriches the accent palette; sRGB stays source of truth. Haptics via lib/haptics.ts (Android-only, no-op on iOS — Safari has no Vibration API).
stack.pwa: public/sw.js is push-only (NO caching SW). UpdateBanner polls public/version.json for new builds.
stack.featureFlag: lib/features.ts READ_ONLY=true is VESTIGIAL — only referenced in a doc comment, gates nothing; features check isSupabaseConfigured + per-migration existence instead.

nextApp.deploy: PR -> merge to main -> Vercel auto-deploy. Cache-Control must-revalidate on every route so PWAs pick up fresh builds; version.json + UpdateBanner nudge stale installs.
nextApp.buildId: next.config.ts stamps NEXT_PUBLIC_BUILD_ID from VERCEL_GIT_COMMIT_SHA and writes public/version.json from the same source.

mediaServer.port: 8790 (8787 is owned by an unrelated Innjoy process — do not collide)
mediaServer.storage: local filesystem MEDIA_DIR (Supabase never holds media bytes)
mediaServer.deploy: PR -> merge -> git pull on mini -> npm install if deps changed -> launchctl kickstart -k gui/<uid>/com.mlr.media-server -> verify logs. In-process senders don't show in ps. Owner-only remote restart is available in-app (Admin -> Media server).
mediaServer.companions: com.mlr.fm-service (Swift/Vapor 'MLR Assistant', 127.0.0.1:8788), Apple `fm serve` CLI (127.0.0.1:8799, AI moderation), com.mlr.embed-service (Swift/Vapor, 127.0.0.1:8786, NaturalLanguage text embeddings for semantic search).

database.migrations: hand-applied SQL files in supabase/migrations/*.sql (158 files as of this pass, numbered 0001-0154 with some duplicate numbering) pasted into the Supabase SQL editor per-file. DB changes are handed to Brian as SQL, NOT run autonomously (standing rule). pg_cron is enabled and drives scheduled broadcasts + fest sign-up reminders — no external cron needed.

domainGlossary.MLR: Muskellunge Lake Resort — the umbrella app/brand. Forest-green cabin-in-the-pines logo, light-mode only, EST 1987. The universal baseline: everyone is always MLR.
domainGlossary.Family Fest: The one-week annual family gathering — a built-in section at /family-fest/* with its own Renaissance/parchment theme (Cinzel font) + sub-nav. Has a 4-phase 'season' (off-season -> planning -> live -> wrap) from lib/festSeason.ts, mirrored byte-for-byte in the retiring standalone family-fest repo.
domainGlossary.House: A family-branch group (e.g. 'MJT House'). Admin-assigned, at most one per member (profiles.house_id FK). Gets private chat, scoped work items, a shared stay calendar, and a House Hub (/house).
domainGlossary.Committee: A volunteer group (e.g. Beautification, Family Fest). Admin-managed taxonomy (create/rename/archive committees + roles, migration 0112) with a DB roster (committee_roster) + DB membership (committee_members) that gates chat. Family Fest members have 'roles[]' = areas, each its own chat channel.
domainGlossary.Committee Lead: A role suffix ' · Lead' — can organize meetings and edit their own fest items.
domainGlossary.Work item / Work Checklist: A to-do list (WorkChecklist.tsx), MLR-wide or house-scoped, each with urgency (asap/this_year/nice_to_have), optional media + comment thread.
domainGlossary.Cabin / 'place to stay': A bookable lodging unit (cabins table), kind cabin|house, optional named rooms, per-place approver (need not be an admin, migration 0114), owner-to-guest messaging (migration 0120).
domainGlossary.Tee times: A hand-off (not in-app booking) from Local Places to Inshalla CC's foreUP booking via /tee-times.
domainGlossary.Dues: Family Fest payment tiers (flat or per-day), calculated by FestDuesCalculator, paid via Venmo/Zelle/etc deep links in PayView. No payment processor.
domainGlossary.Ask for Help: A member at the resort posts a request; willing+present members get pinged. 'At the resort' presence is inferred (no GPS) from event RSVPs ±2 days or approved cabin stays. Urgent bypasses targeting.
domainGlossary.Family polls: Any signed-in member can create a poll (2-10 options); one changeable vote each. Distinct from 'quick polls in chat' (below), which live inline in a chat room's timeline.
domainGlossary.Quick polls in chat: iMessage-style single/multi-select polls any member can drop into a committee/house chat message stream (migration 0149) — anonymous or attributed results, SQL-enforced anonymity (chat_poll_votes has zero read grants).
domainGlossary.Meeting scheduler: Doodle/when2meet-style tool pinned to a committee/house chat, or family-wide, finalizing into a Google Meet link or a real calendar Event (migrations 0116-0122).
domainGlossary.Event sign-up slots: Limited-capacity time-slot sign-ups on a schedule event or anytime activity — interval, arbitrary slots, or headcount mode, with optional fixed-size teams (migrations 0135-0143).
domainGlossary.Tournament brackets: Single-elim / round-robin / pools-then-bracket competition running on top of an activity's sign-ups or a private activity's roster (migrations 0144-0154).
domainGlossary.Private activities: A member-created, invite-only one-off get-together in the Events tab, visible only to invited people, optionally with its own tournament (migration 0150-0151).
domainGlossary.Conversation search: On-device semantic + keyword search across every conversation a member can see (Feed, committee/area chats, house chat), RLS-scoped per searcher (migrations 0129-0131).
domainGlossary.Home callouts: Admin-managed temporary swipe-away announcement cards on Home (home_callouts), distinct from the permanent Family Fest spotlight and the Announcement banner.
domainGlossary.Announcement banner: Admin notices at the top of the app, auto-expiring; unified with push/email/Activity under AdminBroadcastComposer.
domainGlossary.Preview As / View As: Admin-only, device-local, UI-only role override to check the privacy wall as a member/guest. Never touches the real session, never persists.

routes.purpose: Home — lean priority-ordered feed + always-visible quick-actions grid
Feed tab — resort Posts feed + committee/house chat list + conversation search, switched by internal state (NOT sub-routes)
routes.deepLinks: ?post=<id>, ?c=<slug>&area=<area>, ?house=<slug>, ?m=<messageId>, ?meeting=<id> read client-side to pick the initial pane

routes.purpose: Family Fest overview — season hero + week accordion (incl. anytime events + inline tournament sections)
routes.renders: FestCover, FestStatus, FestRsvp, FestDuesCallout, FestCommitteesLink, FestWeek

routes.purpose: Dinners index — reads like a weekly menu, no click-through
routes.auth: public; edit needs canEditFest or chef/crew

routes.purpose: Standalone dinner detail — ORPHANED (nothing links here), kept for legacy links
Dues calculator + Venmo/PayPal hand-off
Admin/committee editor — schedule, dinners, dues, callouts, sign-ups, tournaments
Desktop master editor opened from iOS app — BARE ROUTE (app chrome suppressed via lib/bareRoutes.ts)
Full-week schedule — ORPHANED from FamilyFestNav (Overview shows same accordion), still directly reachable
Standalone schedule-event detail incl. sign-up slots + tournament section
Activity tab — durable feed of everything involving you, deep-links out
Identity, avatar, contact/pay, notif prefs, text size, sign out; Admin RowLink if admin
Full resort calendar + RSVP + family-wide date polling ('Propose dates') + private-activity list/create
routes.auth: public browse; RSVP/create-activity needs sign-in; propose-family-poll/create-event admin-only

routes.purpose: Committees index
One committee: roster + chat entry + meeting scheduler
routes.params: slug — generateStaticParams unions seed ∪ DB; dynamicParams=true

routes.purpose: Standalone full-screen committee General chat
Member directory + email-a-group
Family voting booth (distinct from in-chat quick polls)
House Hub — calendar/chat/to-do for the viewer's house
Full house calendar (month grid + agenda + resort-event overlay)
MJT House's own fest-dues screen
Ask for Help log
Request a cabin/house stay; admin book-on-behalf; approver review inline; message-guests entry point
Nearby businesses w/ quick action pills
In-app Inshalla CC tee-time hand-off
'Take the tour' — plain-English guide w/ jump pills
Help & how-to — human escape hatch + FAQ + install + text-size
Placeholder — dates coming soon, links to Resort Maintenance committee
Admin dashboard — 10-card grid + Family Fest Planner + Google Form banner
Member directory admin: promote/demote, remove, override profile
Merged banner/Activity/email composer + scheduled queue + Home callout cards
Report/moderation queue for posts, comments, and chat
Committee/role taxonomy CRUD + join-request queues
House CRUD + member assignment
Cabin/place booking queue + place/room CRUD + guest messaging
Edit the public help-contact singleton (resort_config)
Recent sign-in audit log
Owner-only mac-mini media-server status + remote pull-and-restart
Device-local 'view as member/guest' override
Pre-create an account + send a branded magiclink for someone who isn't signed up yet

tabs.notes: TABS array is the single source of truth. Family Fest tab shows a live-dot during isLive/isWrap + wears --color-fest. Activity shows a red unread badge (useUnreadNotifications). Bar auto-hides when the keyboard opens. Icons are hand-rolled SVG (Icon.tsx), no emoji chrome.

header.notes: Renders ONLY on Home (usePathname()==='/'); null elsewhere (each route carries its own h1/BackLink). Centered green MLR cabin logo, sized via a CSS clamp refined at runtime against a 'fit anchor'. id='app-logo' is the SplashIntro FLIP landing target.

components: BackButton (button-twin in FeedView for in-place chat close)
backNav.notes: ScrollReset resets #app-scroll on every route change. Chat rooms in FeedView are NOT routes — 'Back' is a state transition (active->'list') animated via chat-push/chat-pop CSS.

keyNavEdges: Home HouseHubCard -> /house  (then HouseHub 'House chat' card -> /posts?house=<slug>)  [TWO HOPS to house chat — still the open tunnel gap]
/house HubCard -> /posts?house=<slug> (FeedView boot effect opens the room directly), and -> /house/calendar?house=<slug>
FeedView Chats list -> in-place house/committee chat (URL unchanged); ⋯ -> ChatMembersSheet -> MeetingComposer / ChatEmailSheet; '+' composer menu -> ChatPollComposer
/events 'Create an activity' -> PrivateActivityComposer -> PrivateActivitySheet -> optional TournamentSetupSheet

providers.name: DemoDateProvider
providers.purpose: Device-local 'pretend today is X' override for testing fest-season phases + all 'today' filters. Returns null now/today until mounted (SSR-safe).

providers.name: MotionProvider
providers.purpose: framer-motion MotionConfig(reducedMotion="user") — the physics-motion opt-out umbrella for the whole app, mounted once in layout.tsx.

providers.name: IdentityProvider
providers.purpose: Passwordless Supabase email-OTP identity. Exposes { user, userId, isAdmin, authReady, previewAsId, updateUser, promptSignIn, needsIntro, completeIntro, signOut }. Hosts InstallFirstNudge, SignInGate, WelcomeIntro overlays. Implements admin Preview-As (UI-only, never touches real session).

providers.name: Guard (useGuest/PrivateName/Protected/SignInWall)
providers.purpose: Privacy-wall layer (consumes IdentityProvider). SignInWall = whole-screen member gate; Protected = inline '🔒 Sign in' chip; PrivateName = first-name-only for guests; useGuest()=isSupabaseConfigured && !user.

providers.name: AdminGuard
providers.purpose: Per-route admin gate: skeleton while authReady false, 'Admins only' card for non-admins, else children.

providers.name: appReady
providers.purpose: First-paint readiness registry — markPending on cold loads, onQuietOnce holds the splash until it drains (or 4.5s cap).

providers.name: bareRoutes
providers.purpose: useIsBareRoute -> true for /family-fest/master so SplashIntro skips chrome for the iOS-embedded editor.

homeComposition: HomeSpotlight = CalloutStack (swipeable admin home_callouts, each optionally carrying a Sign-up deep-link) over permanent FamilyFestSpotlight (season-aware)
UpcomingEvents (nearest event + inline RSVP; skips Family Fest during its takeover)
HomeQuickActions (always-visible 2-col grid: Events, Committees, People, Ask for Help, Local Places, Cabin Stay)

coreModules.exports: supabase, isSupabaseConfigured (null when env unset -> app builds read-only)

RESORT, FAMILY_FEST, RESORT_EVENTS, SCHEDULE, THINGS_TO_DO, DINNERS, PAYEES, COMMITTEES, FAMILY_FEST_AREAS, POSTS — client-only seed/offline fallback, never the only source once a table exists. NO PII (names/roles only).

useCachedResource(key, empty, fetcher, {persist?, ttlMs?}), readPersisted, writePersisted, removePersisted, clearAllCaches — THE loading-stability primitive (memory Map + optional localStorage/sessionStorage, prefix mlr.cache.v1., 24h TTL, 200KB cap, in-flight dedup).

useSheetDismiss, useBusyAction, useDebouncedCallback, useSaveStatus, useMediaPicker, useUnreadNotifications, useManagedCommittee, useEvents, useHelpRequests, useResolvedHouse, useHouseCalendar, useCanEditFest (cached fest-edit affordance, un-flickers nav-gated states), useTypingChannel (chat typing on its own realtime channel), useTournament (per-item, uid-scoped SWR + realtime over 4 tables), usePrivateActivities (uid-scoped SWR + realtime)
User, House, HouseStay, WorkItem, Post, ResortEvent, EventAttendance, HelpRequest, Cabin, CabinBooking, PushType, NotifType, DEFAULT_PUSH_TYPES, DEFAULT_NOTIF_TYPES
formatDate, formatDateLong, formatTime, timeAgo, formatDateRange, relativeDays, phoneDigits, formatPhoneNational, plural, groupByDay — the ONE place formatting lives
getCurrentUserId, fetchProfiles, fetchGuestProfiles (public_profiles view), fetchJoinState, fetchMyAreas, fetchMyCommitteeRole — App Admin > Committee Lead > Member tiers
fetchResortConfig, RESORT_CONFIG_FALLBACK — help-contact singleton, public-read by design
inviteMember, setMemberEmail, inviteByEmailLink — proxy the mini for service-role actions
tap/select/etc wrappers over navigator.vibrate — Android-only progressive enhancement, no-op on iOS (no Vibration API in Safari)

types, pure bracket math (bracketSize/seedOrder/firstRoundPreview/bracketSummary/computeStandings), fetch+RPC wrappers, applyMatchResult optimistic transform, fetchTournamentsForHost/createTournamentForHost/importEntrantsForHost (polymorphic TournamentHost = {kind:'schedule'|'activity', id})
CRUD + roster + RSVP wrappers for private_activities/private_activity_members, degrades to none on 42P01
useChatPolls(scope) hook, create/vote/close/delete wrappers, mirrors lib/meetings.ts's degrade-to-empty idiom
client seam for the mini's POST /search (semantic + keyword conversation search)
interval/slots/headcount sign-up config + RPC wrappers, shared by schedule events and (interval/slots only) activities

fetchMeetingsForRoom, applyMyAvailability optimistic transform, createMeeting/setMyAvailability/finalizeMeeting/cancelMeeting/deleteMeeting, fetchCanOrganize, googleCalendarCreateUrl + looksLikeMeetLink
fetchCommittees/fetchCommitteeAreas — DB-driven taxonomy, replacing the old hardcoded FAMILY_FEST_AREAS reads

conventions.caching: useCachedResource everywhere. FOUR HARD RULES: (1) memory seeds read sync in useState initializers; persisted seeds ONLY inside post-mount effects (else hydration mismatch). (2) user-scoped keys embed the auth uid (e.g. feed.<uid>, events.<uid|guest>, chatRoom.<uid>.<slug>|<area>, tournament.<uid>.<itemId>, privateActivities.<uid>); pass key=null while uid unresolved. (3) Preview mode never persists (persist: undefined, preview id in a memory-only key). (4) signOut() calls clearAllCaches().
conventions.reads: Direct supabase.from(table).select() from domain modules, guarded by RLS. Every module degrades (never throws): no config -> seed/empty; 42P01 missing table / PGRST202 missing RPC -> seed/[]/false. Several features merge a live DB read with the lib/data.ts seed.
conventions.writes: Auth-sensitive / business-rule writes go through SECURITY DEFINER RPCs, never raw client insert (set_event_attendance, request_cabin_stay, create_committee, cast_poll_vote, create_meeting, send_broadcast_notification, mark_work_item_done, record_match_result, create_private_activity). Simple own-row tables (reactions, home_callout_completions, work_item_comments) write directly (RLS is the gate). Service-role actions (invite, set-email) proxy the mini.
conventions.roles: Three tiers server-enforced: App Admin (profiles.is_admin — DB is single source of truth, NO client allow-list, column-level GRANT blocks self-escalation) > Committee Lead (committee_members.role='Lead' or roster roles[] ending ' Lead', scoped) > Member. Houses have no Lead. Cabin approver (cabins.approver_user_id) and tournament/sign-up managers (lead/crew predicates) are narrow non-admin delegations.
conventions.tenantConfig: Single-tenant. resort_config = admin-editable help contact (public-read). Broader identity is lib/data.ts seed, progressively made live via fest_* content tables + home_callouts + app_images (read via festContent.ts / appImages.ts, always seed-fallback).

domainModules: announcements (legacy seam, superseded by live announcements table)
cabins (stay requests, rooms, approvers, messaging guests)
media (uploadToMini, compressImage, moderatePostText — talks to mini)
privateActivities (invite-only one-off get-togethers + polymorphic tournament host)
search (semantic + keyword conversation search, mini proxy)
tournaments (bracket math + RPC wrappers, polymorphic host)

components.note: 168 components total (grew from 147 since the last pass). Grouped by domain. Shared-UI primitives are the reuse foundation. Chat = HouseChat + CommitteeChat remain separate, though shared HOOKS (useTypingChannel, useCanEditFest) now cross-cut both — full extraction into one shared chat core is still open (see improvementOpportunities W1).

endpoints: GET /f/<path> (public static media, 365d immutable, Range support)
POST /upload?category=posts|chat|work&room=<slug> (requireUser, 30/hr — multer -> magic-byte sniff -> transcode video -> Tier-2 AI moderation -> {url,name,type,path,moderation})
POST /search (requireUser — embeds the query on the mini, calls search_conversations RPC as the caller so RLS scopes results, no service-role for search)
POST /admin/restart-media-server (requireOwner — fast-forward git merge + conditional npm install + process.exit; launchd KeepAlive relaunches)
mediaServer.senders: push-sender.js (web-push/VAPID -> push_subscriptions), apns-sender.js (raw HTTP/2 ES256 -> apns_subscriptions), alert-mailer.js (nodemailer SMTP/Gmail), birthday-notifier.js + work-followup.js (reuse apns delivery). All run IN-PROCESS inside the one node PID; verify via log lines, not ps. All listen on Supabase Realtime with the service-role key.
mediaServer.moderation: moderation.js -> local `fm serve` (127.0.0.1:8799), MOD_MODELS='pcc,system'. sharp downscales a COPY to <=1024px before base64 (fixes 413). Video: ffmpeg samples up to 6 frames. FAIL-OPEN everywhere; Report + admin queue are the backstop. Flagged verdict -> media_moderation table -> trigger holds parent post OR chat message (0128).
mediaServer.search: embed-service (Swift/Vapor, NLContextualEmbedding, 512-d L2-normalized vectors, loopback :8786, separate process from fm-service so an FM SIGTRAP can't take search down) + search-indexer.js (reconciles content_embeddings every ~2 min, indexes ALL statuses — search-time RLS is the only scoping).
mediaServer.note: media-server/fm-service/ is a SEPARATE Swift/Vapor 'MLR Assistant' (127.0.0.1:8788) whose referenced lib/assistant/generate.ts does NOT exist in this checkout — looks unwired/removed, distinct from the `fm serve` moderation mechanism.

backend.apiRoutes: NONE. `find app -name route.ts` is empty. All backend I/O is (a) direct Supabase client calls under RLS, or (b) browser fetch() to the mini's REST endpoints. The Next app has no server API surface.

dataModel.note: ~65 tables (grew from ~55). Writes almost always via SECURITY DEFINER RPCs. Migration 0081 = the RLS lockdown (many tables went members-only). Guests read a masked public_profiles view (first-name only). 158 migration files as of this pass (0001-0154, with some duplicate numbering — ordering is not strictly by filename). For the FULL column-level schema, every RLS policy verbatim, every RPC's exact signature/gate/grant, and a feature-by-feature access matrix, see docs/DATA_MODEL.json — the summaries below are intentionally abbreviated.
identity: profiles (id=auth.users FK; display_name, avatar_url, phone, contact_email, venmo/zelle/cashapp/paypal/apple_cash, pay_preferred, contact_preferred, push_types[], notif_types[], is_admin, house_id, birthday, address, intro_seen, joined_at, mjt_dues_paid_year) — members-only read (0081); is_admin/house_id NEVER client-writable (column GRANT + insert guard)
public_profiles (VIEW — id, first-name-only, avatar_url; security_invoker off so guests bypass profiles RLS by design)
push_subscriptions (own rows; mini service-role reads all)
admin_override / admin_override_votes (zero policies — two-admin 'break glass' via RPCs only)
social: posts (status visible|pending|hidden; members-only read filtered by status; status not self-escalatable)
post_media (trigger holds post if media_moderation flags)
committee_messages (area NULL=General; reply_to_id; deleted_at soft-delete; status visible|pending|hidden 0128; 24h author edit / admin anytime enforced in RLS)
house_messages (+ _media/_reactions/_mentions, house_reads) — mirror committee_message_* 1:1, gated on is_house_member()

chat_polls / chat_poll_options / chat_poll_votes (inline in-chat quick polls; votes table has NO select grant at all — deny-all RLS, tallies come from denormalized vote_count/respondent_count columns)
reactions tables are generic (toggleReaction takes table+idColumn)
houses: house_stays (start/end_date, guest_names[], note — is_house_member read, create/update/delete RPCs)
events: events (stable TEXT id or seed slug; kind, day_rsvp, start_time, source, source_meeting_id; public read; admin RPCs)
event_attendance (event_id text; status, days jsonb, confirmed 0122; members read; set_event_attendance upsert own row)
workItems: work_items (house_id null=MLR-public even to guests; urgency; completed_by/at)
cabins: cabin_bookings (status, decision/edit/cancel email-claim cols, booked_by, request_notify; RPC-only writes; REPLICA IDENTITY FULL)
help: help_requests (presence-derived targeting snapshot, work_item_id link, followup)
broadcasts: announcements (severity, show_banner 0126, event_id/exclude_not_attending, email_sent_at claim; public read; alert_recipients RPC service-role)
notifications (recipient_id; NO client insert — trigger/RPC only; seen_at/read_at/expires_at)
scheduled_broadcasts (kind, payload jsonb incl. reminder sourceType/sourceId/onlyUnconfirmed/excludeCalloutDone, fired by pg_cron run_scheduled_broadcasts)
polls: poll_votes (composite PK — one vote/member; cast_poll_vote RPC)
meetings: meetings (scope committee|house|family; status; meet_url; created_event_id; notify_email/proposal_email_sent_at/confirm_email_sent_at; can_organize_meeting gate)
fest: fest_schedule_items (lead_user_id/crew_user_ids[], self-edit RLS, anytime flag, signup_mode/signup_capacity/signup_team_size, tournament_enabled)
fest_activities (lead/crew — LEGACY: web-retired 0141 in favor of anytime fest_schedule_items, but table is left in place and iOS STILL reads it — see iosParity driftRisks)
fest_activity_slots / fest_activity_signups (parallel isolated implementation of the schedule sign-up tables, for the legacy activities path)
fest_signup_reminders_sent (dedupe ledger for per-slot reminder pushes)
tournaments: tournaments (polymorphic: schedule_item_id XOR private_activity_id; format single_elim|round_robin|pools_bracket; status setup|live|complete; tiebreakers[])
tournament_entrants (solo or team; seed, pool, signup_team_id back-link)
tournament_participants (entrant_id null=pre-team pool; user_id null=account-less typed name)
tournament_matches (round/position, next_match_id+next_slot progression pointer, stage pool|bracket)
privateActivities: private_activity_members (roster: user_id null=typed name, role host|player, optional rsvp)
search: content_embeddings (locked — no anon/authenticated grant, deny-all RLS; search_conversations() SECURITY DEFINER RPC is the only access path, re-applies can_access_committee_area/is_house_member/status/deleted_at gates as the calling member)
config: resort_config (help contact, PUBLIC read by design)
home_callouts (links jsonb, dismiss_id, deadline_at, event_id, signup_item_id 0137)

clientBackendBridge: lib/push.ts -> browser Push API + push_subscriptions table (delivery is on the mini)
lib/moderation.ts -> report_content RPC (client Tier-0/1 only; mini does Tier-2 AI separately)
lib/broadcast.ts -> announcements insert + send_broadcast_notification RPC (mini mailer/senders pick up via Realtime)
lib/scheduledBroadcasts.ts -> scheduled_broadcasts CRUD (pg_cron fires; mini + app are bystanders)
lib/search.ts -> mini POST /search (embeds query -> calls search_conversations as the caller)

chat.summary: The make-or-break feature. Two rooms — HouseChat + CommitteeChat — remain separate components (not yet extracted into one shared core — see improvementOpportunities W1) but now share cross-cutting hooks (useTypingChannel, useCanEditFest). Hosted by FeedView (Feed tab) inside a viewport-pinned, keyboard-aware fixed container with chat-push/chat-pop slide, plus a chat-unmask hold-then-fade cover masking the room-open paint on mobile. Feature-rich: media/reactions/@mentions/replies/24h edit+soft-delete/unread/meeting bar/quick polls, NOW WITH optimistic send + typing indicators + smart scroll.

dataFlow.storage: house_messages / committee_messages (+ _media/_reactions/_mentions) + house_reads/committee_area_reads (room-level read marker only). chat_polls merge into the same timeline client-side.
dataFlow.send: OPTIMISTIC as of this pass (previously NOT optimistic — CONFIRMED FIXED): instant send, the bubble appears immediately with a spring-in animation, reconciled against the realtime echo. A jump pill handles 'new message while scrolled up'.
dataFlow.typing: SHIPPED (previously 'NONE' — CONFIRMED FIXED): useTypingChannel (lib/hooks.ts) drives a TypingIndicator component over its own Supabase Realtime broadcast channel, separate from the message tables.
dataFlow.liveUpdates: Supabase Realtime postgres_changes on the message/media/reaction/mention tables feeds the room; the exact patch-vs-full-refetch strategy post-optimistic-send rework was not re-verified line-by-line this pass — treat improvementOpportunities W3 (incremental realtime patching) as UNCONFIRMED rather than assume it's fixed alongside send/typing.
dataFlow.readReceipts: room-level only (house_reads/committee_area_reads last_read_at) — drives Feed unread badge. No per-message Read/Delivered confirmed; unread-divider status also unconfirmed this pass (see improvementOpportunities P2).

confirmedFixedSinceLastPass: No optimistic send -> FIXED (instant send + spring-in bubbles)
Scroll-to-bottom on new msg was an instant jump -> FIXED ('smart scroll + jump pill' per CLAUDE.md)
No haptics -> FIXED for Android (lib/haptics.ts), correctly remains a no-op on iOS Safari

stillOpenOrUnconfirmed.gap: HouseChat + CommitteeChat are still two near-duplicated components, not a shared chat core
Every mutation possibly still triggers a broader-than-necessary refetch (incl. the profiles table) — not re-verified this pass
No unread divider inside a room confirmed shipped
No draft persistence per room confirmed shipped
Chat attachments possibly still stack vertically instead of reusing MediaGrid carousel
No 1:1 direct messages — only group rooms (committee/house)

In-room-only message search is superseded by the broader Conversation search feature (0129-0131), which searches across ALL rooms a member can see — treat P7 as satisfied by a bigger feature, not literally built as originally scoped

majorFeatures.note: Substantial feature areas that either didn't exist or weren't documented as of the last architecture pass (2026-07-22). Each entry names its migrations, key files, and iOS-parity status (cross-referenced against the iosParity section's Explore-agent findings, not just CLAUDE.md's own annotations — see driftRisks where they disagree).

eventSignupSlots.summary: A schedule event or anytime activity can take limited sign-ups for time slots — three modes (interval/derived, slots/arbitrary list, headcount/no time dimension), optional fixed-size teams, custom required fields, per-slot reminder pushes via pg_cron.
eventSignupSlots.iosParity: web-only, shared schema/RPCs — CONFIRMED zero iOS presence (no MeetingScheduler-style false-negative here; the iOS repo has no sign-up UI at all).

tournamentBrackets.summary: Single-elimination / round-robin / pools-then-bracket tournaments attached to a fest activity OR a private activity, drawing entrants from that item's sign-ups/roster. Winner-only scoring (scores optional), recursive advance-cascade on result changes, advisory-lock-serialized manager edits.
tournamentBrackets.iosParity: CONFIRMED zero iOS presence — grep across the entire mlr-app-ios repo for 'tournament'/'Tournament' returns no matches. No Models/Services/Views exist.

privateActivities.summary: Member-created, invite-only one-off get-togethers in the Events tab — private RLS (creator/roster/admin only), reuse the polymorphic tournament system, notify only opted-in invitees.
privateActivities.iosParity: CONFIRMED zero iOS presence — grep for 'PrivateActivity'/'private_activit' returns no matches. Activities/ folder in iOS only has a thin 94-line generic ActivitiesView, unrelated to this feature.

quickPollsInChat.summary: iMessage-style single/multi-select polls droppable into a committee/house chat's message timeline (not a pinned bar) via the composer's '+' menu; anonymous or attributed results, SQL-enforced anonymity (chat_poll_votes has zero SELECT grant, tallies via denormalized trigger-maintained counters).
quickPollsInChat.iosParity: CONFIRMED zero iOS presence — grep for 'ChatPoll'/'chat_poll' returns no matches. iOS Polls/ folder implements only the separate family-polls feature (351-line PollsView.swift).

meetingScheduling.summary: Doodle/when2meet-style scheduler pinned to a committee/house chat room's ⋯ menu, or family-wide from /events ('Propose dates', admin-only). Proposes slots -> members vote Yes/If-need-be/No -> organizer finalizes to a Google Meet link (guided, no OAuth) or creates a real calendar Event with unconfirmed-carried-over RSVPs.
meetingScheduling.iosParity: BUILT, NOT A GAP — see iosParity.docVsRealityDrift: CLAUDE.md and the web repo's own docs/ios-parity-2026-07.md both describe this as 'planned follow-up' / 'entirely missing (P0)' on iOS, but the Explore-agent survey found MeetingComposer.swift (401 lines), MeetingSchedulerSheet.swift (495 lines), MeetingSectionBar.swift (111 lines), and Services/MeetingsService.swift (495 lines) already substantively implemented in mlr-app-ios. Verify current behavior in Xcode before assuming this is unbuilt.

conversationSearch.summary: On-device (mini) semantic + keyword search across every conversation a member can see (Feed posts+comments, committee/area chats, house chat). RLS-safe via a SECURITY DEFINER RPC that re-resolves auth.uid() as the caller. Ranking is keyword-precise (Postgres full-text @@, NOT ts_rank(...)>0) — pure semantic search is intentionally off (Apple's mean-pooled vectors are too anisotropic to threshold; they only break ties among keyword matches).
conversationSearch.iosParity: CONFIRMED zero iOS presence. The iOS Search/ folder has only a 144-line GlobalSearchView.swift that is a generic search UI unrelated to search_conversations — grep for 'search_conversations' returns no matches.

placesToStayApproverAndMessaging.summary: Admins can add arbitrary new bookable places (not just the original two), each with kind cabin|house and an optional per-place approver who need not be an app admin. That approver (or an admin) can broadcast a message to everyone currently/soon staying at their place.
placesToStayApproverAndMessaging.iosParity: LIKELY BUILT per the iOS survey — CabinMessageSheet.swift and AdminCabinDetails equivalents exist in the iOS Cabins/ folder (CabinBookingsView, AdminCabinDetails, CabinMessageSheet, EditCabinBookingSheet, PickMyRoomSheet all present), matching the web feature set closely. Not exhaustively line-by-line verified this pass.

committeeAdminTaxonomy.summary: Admins create/rename/archive committees and their roles (areas) in-app instead of the taxonomy being hardcoded; renames cascade through 7 denormalized places in one transaction.
committeeAdminTaxonomy.iosParity: BUILT — CommitteeService.swift (778 lines) includes archive/restore + rename RPC calls, plus a dedicated MyCommitteeAreasSheet.swift. Confirmed via docs/ios-committee-taxonomy-parity.md and file survey.

chefCrewSelfEdit.summary: A dinner/schedule-event/activity's assigned chef/lead + crew can edit that item's operational details (not day/title/who's-assigned) without full committee/admin access — a second, narrower RLS policy layered on the blanket can_edit_fest() write policy.
chefCrewSelfEdit.iosParity: Not explicitly confirmed this pass — iOS FamilyFestTab has FestDinnerEditSheet/FestActivityEditSheet/FestInlineEditSheets which suggest some parity, but the narrower self-edit-vs-full-edit RLS distinction was not verified.

iosParity.summary: Payload for opening Xcode on mlr-app-ios: what exists, how it's organized, what's confirmed missing, and where the project's own docs disagree with the actual repo. Built from an Explore-agent survey of /home/user/mlr-app-ios plus cross-referencing CLAUDE.md's inline '📱' parity annotations and the repo's own docs/ios-*.md notes (read from the mlr-app side).
iosParity.sharedBackend: iOS talks to the exact same Supabase project as web — no separate database, no separate RPCs. A schema/RPC/realtime change made for web must be checked against iOS consumers (Services/*.swift, Models/*.swift) before shipping, and vice versa.

repoStructure.MLRApp: The main iOS app target (SwiftUI, ~184 Swift files). Organized by feature module (see featureModules) plus App/ (AppEnvironment.swift Supabase client setup, MLRApp.swift @main, RootView.swift root nav shell), Tabs/ (5 tab modules mirroring web's TabBar), Models/ (16 files, one per major table family), Services/ (16 files, one per web lib/*.ts data-access seam), Native/ (EventKit/Contacts/CoreSpotlight/Haptics/MapKit/Messages/PassKit bridges — iOS-only), Intents/ (20 files — Siri/Shortcuts/App Intents surface, iOS-only), Weather/ (WeatherKit event badges, iOS-only), LiveActivities/ (ActivityKit Fest countdown controller, iOS-only).
repoStructure.MLRCore: A tiny separate Swift Package (Package.swift + 2 files, ~32 lines total) — a thin shared-code seam intended to be importable from both the main app and the widget extension, though most Supabase logic actually lives in MLRApp/App/AppEnvironment.swift directly rather than here.
repoStructure.MLRWidget: WidgetKit extension target: a Family Fest countdown widget (small/medium + Lock Screen accessory), a 'Next at the Resort' widget, and the ActivityKit Live Activity UI for Fest week.
repoStructure.AppGroup: Shared app-group storage (group.com.muskellungelakeresort.mlr) so the main app and widget extension exchange a data snapshot without a network round trip.
repoStructure.iosCompatConstraints: IOS_COMPAT.md documents hard rules: iOS 18 is the minimum deployment target; Liquid Glass (iOS 26) visuals are always gated behind `if #available` via Shared/Design/LiquidGlass.swift helpers; on-device FoundationModels/AI is explicitly BANNED (crashes pre-26); `IndexedEntity` conformance is FORBIDDEN (crashes at launch on iOS 18 despite compiling clean).

featureModulesBuilt: Cabins (full booking lifecycle: AdminCabinDetails, CabinBookingsView, CabinMessageSheet, CabinRequestSheet, EditCabinBookingSheet, PickMyRoomSheet — matches web's places-to-stay feature closely)
Committees (chat + roster + self-service area management + email-a-group + admin taxonomy CRUD)
Houses (chat, calendar, Hub, MJT dues — full parity per CLAUDE.md's own note)
Help / HelpRequests (guide, how-to, Ask for Help)
Meetings (MeetingComposer 401 lines, MeetingSchedulerSheet 495 lines, MeetingSectionBar 111 lines, MeetingsService 495 lines — SUBSTANTIAL, see docVsRealityDrift)
People (directory, admin member/roster management, email pools)
Polls (family polls — 351-line PollsView.swift, single-file but complete: create/vote/close/delete)
Work Items (composer, detail sheet, checklist section)
Home tab (delight cards doctrine matches web: weather, on-this-day, admin dashboard shortcut, callout stack, work checklist card)
Family Fest tab (largest module, 14 files: planner, crew view, dinner/activity/schedule edit sheets, dues calculator, photos, status)

Profile/Admin tab (14-file admin console: broadcast composer, cabin bookings, callouts, committees, help contact, houses, invite, members, moderation, notification composer, scheduled broadcasts, sign-ins, app-images, preview-as — a fairly complete native mirror of web's /admin dashboard)

iOS-only superpowers (no web equivalent): App Intents/Siri Shortcuts (20 files — ask-for-help, cabin, chat-search, committee-action, fest-countdown, member-query, next-event, personal, place, resort-life, resort-query, search, work-item intents + donations), WeatherKit event badges, ActivityKit Live Activities + widgets, Calendar/Contacts/Spotlight/Maps/Messages native bridges, native haptics

confirmedParityGaps.iosStatus: Zero code — no Models/Services/Views, confirmed by repo-wide grep for 'tournament'/'Tournament'.
confirmedParityGaps.buildNotes: Would need Tournament/TournamentEntrant/TournamentParticipant/TournamentMatch models, a TournamentsService mirroring lib/tournaments.ts's RPC wrappers, and bracket/round-robin/pools UI. The web's polymorphic host pattern (schedule_item_id XOR private_activity_id) should carry over directly since it's the same schema.

confirmedParityGaps.iosStatus: Zero code — the existing ActivitiesView.swift (94 lines) is unrelated (mirrors the legacy fest_activities concept, not this feature).
confirmedParityGaps.buildNotes: Needs a PrivateActivity model + service, roster management UI, and (if tournaments ship) the shared tournament host wiring. Naturally surfaces in the Events tab like on web.

confirmedParityGaps.iosStatus: Zero code — existing Polls/PollsView.swift is the separate family-polls feature, unrelated.
confirmedParityGaps.buildNotes: Needs a ChatPoll model + service and inline-timeline rendering inside CommitteeChatView/HouseChatView's message list (not a separate screen) to match web's 'renders inline, not a pinned bar' design decision.

confirmedParityGaps.iosStatus: Zero code — Search/GlobalSearchView.swift (144 lines) is a generic search UI, confirmed unrelated by grep for 'search_conversations'.
confirmedParityGaps.buildNotes: Could call the same mini POST /search endpoint (which forwards the caller's Supabase token so RLS scopes results) rather than reimplementing embedding logic natively.

confirmedParityGaps.iosStatus: No dedicated sign-up UI found in the survey.
confirmedParityGaps.buildNotes: Would need to mirror lib/scheduleSignups.ts's three modes (interval/slots/headcount) plus team support against fest_schedule_slots/fest_schedule_signups.

docVsRealityDrift.docClaim: Both CLAUDE.md ('📱 iOS parity is a planned follow-up') and the web repo's own docs/ios-parity-2026-07.md (frames WS1 'Meeting scheduler' as P0, 'entirely missing') say this is unbuilt on iOS.
docVsRealityDrift.reality: The Explore-agent survey found substantial, real code: MeetingComposer.swift (401 lines), MeetingSchedulerSheet.swift (495 lines), MeetingSectionBar.swift (111 lines), MeetingsService.swift (495 lines), plus a Meeting model — over 1,400 lines total, not a stub.
docVsRealityDrift.recommendation: Before doing any Meeting-scheduler work in Xcode, actually run the app and check current behavior — the docs are stale, not the code. This is the single biggest doc-vs-reality mismatch found this pass; treat every '📱 no iOS parity' claim as a hypothesis to verify against the live repo, not a fact.

docVsRealityDrift.docClaim: Web retired the standalone 'anytime activities' concept in favor of anytime-flagged fest_schedule_items, but the fest_activities table was deliberately left in place because iOS still reads it.
docVsRealityDrift.reality: Confirmed consistent — iOS's Activities/ActivitiesView.swift (94 lines) is exactly this legacy path. This is an intentional, acknowledged drift point (not a doc error): a web edit to a converted event will NOT reflect on the untouched fest_activities row iOS reads.
docVsRealityDrift.recommendation: When iOS eventually adopts the anytime-fest_schedule_items model (a real follow-up, not done), this dual-read situation resolves. Until then, don't expect the two clients' 'anytime activities' lists to agree.

docVsRealityDrift.docClaim: Not explicitly claimed done or not-done in CLAUDE.md's terse iOS notes for this feature.
docVsRealityDrift.reality: iOS Cabins/ folder already has CabinMessageSheet.swift and AdminCabinDetails-equivalent files, suggesting this landed, consistent with docs/ios-parity-2026-07.md describing WS2 as likely-landed based on file presence.
docVsRealityDrift.recommendation: Spot-check in Xcode rather than assume from file presence alone — file existing != feature fully wired.

iosOwnDocs.ios-swiftui-strategy.md: Foundational strategy doc: 'clean platform split' (web covers Android/desktop, iOS is a fully native additive second client against the SAME Supabase project — RSVPs/photos sync instantly between clients, no separate iOS DB). Full stack: SwiftUI, supabase-swift, Kingfisher, WeatherKit, ActivityKit, App Intents, APNs via apns_subscriptions + apns-sender.js. Documents a 7-phase build plan (Foundation -> Core Tabs -> Fest/Events -> Social/People -> Admin/Polish -> TestFlight -> App Store -> Native Extras), substantially executed per the file survey.
iosOwnDocs.ios-parity-2026-07.md: Most current author note (as of that writing): frames itself as the delta since iOS's last sync (2026-07-18) through web PR #362 / migrations 0114-0128. Claims prior workstreams A-F (design foundation, Fest redesign, cabins parity, broadcasts/reminders, OnThisDay/reactions, committee self-service/taxonomy) all landed — confirmed by the file survey. Lists 8 remaining workstreams: WS1 Meeting scheduler (see docVsRealityDrift — likely already landed despite the doc), WS2 Places-to-stay (likely landed), WS3 Family roster + email pools (likely landed — FamilyRosterService/AdminFamilyRosterView/EmailPoolsView all present), WS4 Unified broadcast composer (likely landed — AdminBroadcastComposer present), WS5 Chat parity (optimistic send/typing/smart-scroll/moderation/Home-to-House-chat tunnel — still a real gap per the survey, and note web ALSO closed several of these same gaps since this doc was written, so re-diff before starting), WS6 Fest Dinners weekly-menu list, WS7 Home/House-Hub IA regrouping, WS8 native motion polish (lowest priority). Explicitly out of scope for iOS: GitHub-Pages/Vercel plumbing, desktop sidebar layout, PWA-specific fixes.
iosOwnDocs.ios-committee-taxonomy-parity.md: Focused handoff spec for the admin-managed committee/role taxonomy (migration 0112) — confirmed landed by the file survey (CommitteeService.swift has archive/restore + rename RPC calls, MyCommitteeAreasSheet.swift exists).
iosOwnDocs.recommendationForDeveloper: Treat these three docs as directional history, not current truth. Before starting any iOS work: (1) grep the actual Swift source for the feature/table name first (as this survey did), (2) only then read the .md notes for context on WHY a decision was made, (3) update the stale .md claims once you've verified reality — the docVsRealityDrift entries above are the known stale spots as of 2026-07-24.

designSystem.identity: LIGHT MODE ONLY, forever. Vintage Northwoods, warm birch page, forest-green brand. Depth from surface color + 1px hairline borders (ring-1 ring-border) + subtle ambient gradient — restrained, iOS-like.

colorTokens.ffSection: parchment theme scoped to .ff-section (bg #f4ecd8, wine #8b2e2e, azure #1e3a8a, Cinzel serif) — re-declares the same --color-* vars

motionTokensExisting.utilities: page-enter (Messages slide-in), chat-push 300ms / chat-pop 240ms, chat-unmask (hold-then-fade room-open cover), scrim-in/out, sheet-panel/close, pop-panel/close, rise (stagger via --i), press (tap scale 0.96 spring), callout-wiggle, splash-logo/motto/wash
motionTokensExisting.reducedMotion: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) collapses all to a 1ms fade — every framer-motion primitive routes through MotionProvider's reducedMotion="user" for the same effect.

designSystem.currentMotionInventory: CSS foundation (page transitions, chat push/pop/unmask, .press everywhere, Lightbox, splash FLIP, callout swipe+wiggle) PLUS framer-motion physics (AnimatedNumber count-up, AnimatedList FLIP reorder, SegmentedControl shared-element pill, Celebration confetti, TypingIndicator, spring-in chat bubbles) PLUS a P3/HDR color layer and Android-only haptics. This is essentially the full motionKitToPort backlog from the prior pass, now shipped — see motionKit.status below.

motionKit.sourceInspiration: Ported from the sibling stock-game app's motion approach (framer-motion physics layered on a CSS foundation, restrained — only ONE box-shadow in that whole app; depth is color steps + hairlines + blur).
motionKit.status: SHIPPED — deps installed (framer-motion ^12.42.2), MotionProvider mounted once in app/layout.tsx, all core primitives live in components/: AnimatedNumber, AnimatedList, SegmentedControl (+ FamilyFestNav layoutId pill), Celebration, TypingIndicator. P3/HDR color layer live in globals.css. Haptics via lib/haptics.ts.

primitives.AnimatedNumber: rAF count-up (tabular-nums), gated by reduced-motion. Live wherever counts render (votes, dues, RSVP tallies, unread badges — exact call sites not re-audited this pass).
primitives.AnimatedList: layout='position' FLIP reorder, spring ~500/40. Live for at least People directory / Feed conversation ordering per the prior plan's target list.
primitives.SegmentedControl: Shared-element layoutId pill — used directly by FamilyFestNav; likely also on AttendanceControl per the original plan (not re-verified line-by-line).
primitives.Celebration: CSS/framer confetti + accent banner, sessionStorage-guarded once-per-event. Target moments per the original plan: RSVP 'Going', poll vote, help 'Covered', dues paid.
primitives.TypingIndicator: Renders over useTypingChannel's realtime broadcast presence in chat rooms.

hdrColor.mechanism: @supports (color: color(display-p3 1 1 1)) upgrade layer over the sRGB hex source (hex stays fallback/source of truth). Neutrals stay sRGB.
hdrColor.applyTo: Richer P3 variants of --color-primary/--color-accent/--color-lake/--color-sun/--color-dusk/--color-fest on capable displays, preserving the exact light Northwoods identity.

hardConstraints: LIGHT MODE ONLY forever. Never add a dark theme. Never use a dark translucent surface tint (bg-black/NN, bg-zinc-*/NN) as a card/panel bg (goes muddy grey) — bg-black/NN is OK only as a modal scrim.
Never hard-code hex in components — add/edit a --color-* token in globals.css @theme.
profiles.is_admin & house_id are NEVER client-writable (column GRANT + insert guard). No client admin allow-list.

RLS is the real privacy gate, not the UI (migration 0081 lockdown). Any new sensitive field/table needs matching RLS. UI hiding is insufficient. chat_poll_votes (0149) and content_embeddings (0129) both go further — zero SELECT grant at all (deny-all), not just members-only.
resort_config help-contact stays PUBLIC-read (it's the sign-in escape hatch). Don't lock it down.
No PII in the client bundle (lib/data.ts seed = names/roles only; lib/help.ts is a neutral placeholder).
Committee/role/cabin 'delete' = archive (archived_at), never destructive. Slugs are immutable.

24h edit / soft-delete window on messages is enforced in RLS, not just UI — preserve server-side. Same status-visible|pending|hidden moderation model now applies to committee_messages/house_messages (0128), not just posts.
Preview/'View as' is UI-only, never touches the real session, never persists (SWR rule 3).

Any schema/RPC/realtime change must preserve iOS app (mlr-app-ios) parity — it reads the same tables. Before assuming a feature is iOS-unbuilt, verify against the live iOS repo, not just CLAUDE.md's inline notes (see iosParity.docVsRealityDrift — at least one 'no iOS parity' claim was found to be stale this pass).
Supabase migrations are handed to Brian as SQL to run; do NOT apply autonomously without explicit authorization.

Respect notification prefs (notif_types/push_types); only help_urgent intentionally bypasses per-category push (signup_reminder is also an override-push, by design — anyone who signed up chose to be reachable).
Do NOT reintroduce pull-to-refresh (deliberately removed — realtime + UpdateBanner cover it).
The SWR cache four rules (memory-sync/storage-in-effect, uid-scoped keys, preview-never-persists, signOut clears) — breaking them causes hydration mismatch or cross-account data bleed.

Tournament match-result changes run a recursive advance-cascade that clears every stale downstream result; concurrent manager edits are serialized with pg_advisory_xact_lock(tournament_id) — don't bypass this lock with a new write path.
Do NOT touch stock-game or innjoy-mobile from this repo — reference only.
When adding a route/dependency/env var/data-model change, update CLAUDE.md + README.md in the same commit.

improvementOpportunities.legend: Each item: id, title, why, files, effort (S/M/L), risk (low/med/high), status. Status reflects this pass's findings: 'shipped' = confirmed done via CLAUDE.md/file checks, 'open' = not confirmed done, 'unconfirmed' = plausibly done but not verified this pass, 'superseded' = solved differently than originally scoped.

newSincePass.title: Resolve the fest_activities dual-read drift
newSincePass.why: Web retired fest_activities in favor of anytime fest_schedule_items (0141) but iOS still reads the legacy table; the two can silently disagree on 'anytime activities' content. Either port iOS to the new model, or explicitly document this as permanent.

implementationPlan.approach: Ship in small, isolated PRs. The prior motion-foundation + chat-core-first waves have substantially executed (see improvementOpportunities statuses). Remaining work splits into two tracks: (a) closing residual web chat/perf gaps, and (b) the iOS parity backlog surfaced by this pass's Explore-agent survey.

waves.name: Chat core + performance
waves.status: PARTIALLY complete — send/typing/scroll shipped, but the shared-core refactor (W1) and incremental-patching (W3) are unconfirmed/open

waves.name: iOS parity closeout (NEW)
waves.rationale: Web has pulled ahead on five features (sign-up slots, tournaments, private activities, chat polls, conversation search) that iOS has zero code for. Recommend building in roughly that order — sign-up slots first since tournaments/private-activities/chat-polls all build on adjacent patterns already proven on iOS (Meetings' scope/RPC-wrapper style, Cabins' sheet-based CRUD style).
items: N6 (reconcile stale docs — do this FIRST, it's S-effort and prevents wasted re-verification)
N7 (fest_activities drift — lower priority, can wait for N5)
